requirements.text: The system must support hierarchical process modeling where each interaction in a collaboration diagram can be decomposed into a sub-process with its own collaboration diagrams and process models, supporting unlimited depth levels while maintaining appropriate abstraction at each level.
acceptance_criteria: Generate Level 0 (high-level) collaboration diagrams
Support decomposition of any interaction into Level N+1 sub-process
Maintain consistent modeling patterns across hierarchy levels
Automatic folder structure generation for each process level

requirements.text: The system must implement boundary concepts with participant stereotypes where only one external actor interacts with a target participant boundary, sub-level collaborations occur within the boundary using Mermaid box syntax, and participant types distinguish different system components (actor, boundary, control, entity).
acceptance_criteria: Use Mermaid sequence diagram box syntax for boundary representation
Enforce single-actor-to-boundary interaction patterns
Generate sub-process diagrams within appropriate boxes
Validate boundary consistency across hierarchy levels
Implement participant stereotypes in diagram generation
Apply decomposition rules: only control participants can be decomposed
Ensure boundary participants are positioned as first recipients within boundaries

requirements.text: Enhance the existing diagram-generatecollaboration skill to support automatic boundary detection and box generation, hierarchical diagram creation with proper nesting, integration with sub-process folder management, and backward compatibility with Project 1 diagram formats.
acceptance_criteria: Extend existing collaboration diagram skill with boundary capabilities
Generate both flat and hierarchical diagram formats
Support migration from Project 1 diagrams to hierarchical format
Maintain all existing diagram generation features

requirements.text: The system must automatically manage folder structures for process hierarchies, creating sub-folders for each decomposed process with consistent naming conventions and cross-reference navigation between hierarchy levels.
acceptance_criteria: Automatic sub-folder creation for decomposed processes
Consistent folder structure: Level-N-ProcessName/
Include standard files: main.md, collaboration.md, process.md, domain-model.md
Cross-reference links between hierarchy levels

requirements.text: Each hierarchy level must maintain appropriate focus with complexity metrics for determining decomposition need, guidelines for appropriate detail at each level, automatic warnings for overly complex diagrams, and decomposition suggestions based on interaction patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Complexity metrics for determining decomposition need
Guidelines for appropriate detail level at each hierarchy
Automatic warnings for overly complex single-level diagrams
Decomposition suggestions based on interaction patterns

requirements.text: All generated artifacts must comply with EDPS methodology including evolutionary development principles support, incremental model refinement capabilities, change impact analysis across hierarchy levels, and traceability between requirements and process levels.
acceptance_criteria: EDPS-compliant process modeling patterns
Support for iterative refinement of process hierarchies
Change propagation across hierarchy levels
Requirements traceability to appropriate process levels

requirements.text: The system must update and evolve the organizational model (OrgModel) to reflect enhanced hierarchical EDPS capabilities, including updating process documents with boundary concepts, enhancing collaboration diagrams with hierarchical decomposition, and integrating boundary patterns into domain models.
acceptance_criteria: Update OrgModel skill development process with hierarchical boundary concepts
Enhance existing organizational collaboration diagrams with box syntax
Create organizational templates for hierarchical process modeling
Ensure organizational methodology reflects Project 3 capabilities
Document organizational boundary patterns and best practices

requirements.text: Enhanced skills must be capable of automatically updating OrgModel documents including detecting when organizational processes need hierarchical decomposition, generating boundary-enhanced collaboration diagrams, and maintaining traceability between skills and organizational model updates.
acceptance_criteria: Skills can identify OrgModel documents requiring hierarchical enhancement
Automatic generation of boundary-enhanced organizational diagrams
Preservation of organizational model consistency and traceability
Integration with existing orgmodel-update skill from Project 1

requirements.text: Maintain compatibility with Project 1 outputs including ability to import and enhance Project 1 collaboration diagrams, migration tools for converting flat diagrams to hierarchical format, preservation of existing requirements and domain model links, and seamless integration with existing skills framework.
acceptance_criteria: Import Project 1 collaboration diagrams without modification
Optional enhancement to hierarchical format
Preserve all existing requirement links and metadata
No breaking changes to existing skill interfaces

requirements.text: The system must classify all participants using defined types (actor, boundary, control, entity), apply decomposition rules based on participant types, maintain type consistency across hierarchy levels, and validate type usage in boundary implementations.
acceptance_criteria: Generate Mermaid diagrams with participant type definitions
Use participant type icons instead of stereotype text
Enforce decomposition rules during sub-process generation
Validate boundary positioning as first recipient within boundaries
Maintain participant type consistency across hierarchy levels
Provide clear error messages for participant type rule violations

requirements.text: Use box syntax for boundary representation (no native boundary element), maintain valid Mermaid sequence diagram syntax, support for nested structure representation within Mermaid limitations.
acceptance_criteria: Valid Mermaid box syntax generation
Diagrams render correctly in VS Code preview
Graceful handling of Mermaid syntax limitations

requirements.text: Maintain seamless GitHub Copilot integration, preserve markdown-based workflow, support for large diagram rendering in VS Code preview.
acceptance_criteria: Seamless GitHub Copilot skill invocation
Markdown-first workflow preserved
Large diagrams render within VS Code without issues

boundary.position_rule: First recipient of actor messages within boundaries

control.position_rule: Only type that can be decomposed into sub-processes

entity.role: Data storage, resources, or passive components
entity.position_rule: Stable resources without internal process decomposition

decomposition_rules: Control-Only Decomposition: Only control-type participants can become sub-processes
Boundary First Reception: Boundary-type participants must receive actor messages first within any boundary box
Actor Externality: Actor-type participants always remain external to boundaries
Entity Stability: Entity-type participants represent stable resources
